aliases: Grilled chicken salad with four toppings

english: Grilled chicken salad with four toppings

ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken salad — composite dish of cooked chicken and mixed salad vegetables, no dressing
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (composite poultry salad dish) used as closest parent match; the specific 4-topping no-sauce customisable variant is not individually coded in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept found for this specific customisable salad preparation.

preparation_method: no cooking required for salad base

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 g, representing a full plate of mixed greens topped with cooked chicken and four vegetable toppings with no sauce or dressing.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Salad (4 Toppings), No Sauce — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chicken Salad (4 Toppings), No Sauce (cr_id F08010013). All nine reported nutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are sourced at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked chicken breast (NDB 05064) and mixed salad greens (NDB 11959) as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed green salad with lean protein
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific customisable chicken salad preparation. GI estimated at 15 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for mixed green salad with lean protein (GI ~15), consistent with the very low carbohydrate content (2.6 g/100g) and high dietary fibre (2.35 g/100g). Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (15 × 7.8) / 100 ≈ 1.2, classified as very low.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (174 mg/100g) with no added sauce or dressing
hypertension.guidance: At 174 mg sodium per 100 g and no sauce, this salad is a suitable low-sodium meal option for hypertensive individuals; patients should ensure chicken is not pre-marinated in high-sodium sauces and should avoid adding salty toppings such as processed cheese or pickled vegetables.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.6 g/100g), high protein (6.8 g/100g), and high dietary fibre (2.35 g/100g) producing a very low glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 15 and a glycemic load of approximately 1.2 per 300 g serving, this dish produces minimal postprandial glucose excursion and is an excellent meal choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; patients should avoid starchy toppings such as corn, croutons, or sweetened dried fruit to preserve the low glycemic profile.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (1.57 g/100g), low estimated saturated fat (~0.45 g/100g), and high dietary fibre (2.35 g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat content, absence of added dressing, and high fibre from mixed greens and vegetable toppings make this dish cardioprotective; individuals with cardiovascular disease should select lean grilled or poached chicken and choose fibre-rich toppings such as tomatoes, cucumber, and leafy greens to maximise LDL-lowering benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Salad (4 Toppings), No Sauce is a Western-style composed salad dish consisting of cooked chicken — typically grilled or poached — served over a bed of fresh mixed salad greens accompanied by a customer's choice of four vegetable or salad toppings, with no sauce or dressing applied. The dish is light, refreshing, and high in protein relative to its caloric density, delivering 6.8 g of protein and only 52 kcal per 100 g. The absence of sauce eliminates added sugar, fat, and sodium that dressings typically contribute, making this one of the cleanest low-calorie meal options available in a customisable salad format. The texture combines the tender, slightly chewy quality of cooked chicken with the crisp freshness of raw salad greens and crunchy vegetable toppings. Nutritionally, the dish is characterised by very low carbohydrate (2.6 g/100g), moderate dietary fibre (2.35 g/100g), low fat (1.57 g/100g), and a sodium level of 174 mg/100g that remains within acceptable limits for sodium-conscious diets. Calcium (28 mg/100g) and iron (0.54 mg/100g) are present from the greens and chicken respectively. Clinically, this dish is well-suited for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low glycemic load, for hypertensive patients due to its low sodium and absence of dressing, and for those with cardiovascular disease due to its low saturated fat and high fibre content. It is classified as halal and gluten-free in its standard preparation. Common topping choices include cucumber, tomato, corn, shredded carrot, red onion, bell pepper, croutons, and cheese, though the no-sauce variant is specifically designed to minimise caloric and glycemic contribution from condiments.
common_user_queries: Is chicken salad good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95 for an exact match to this food item (cr_id F08010013). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA SR Legacy for the closest composite equivalent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for mixed green salad with lean protein, which is the closest defensible approved-source match.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Reason: the HPB Foodo database entry for this food (F08010013) does not report these micronutrient and fatty acid fractions individually. USDA FoodData Central does not contain an exact match for this specific customisable 4-topping no-sauce chicken salad preparation. Detailed fatty acid fractions and full micronutrient panel are not available in any approved source for this exact composite dish as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat (0.45 g/100g) is estimated from USDA SR Legacy NDB 05064 (chicken breast, cooked, roasted) and NDB 11959 (mixed salad greens) weighted at approximately 40:60 ratio by mass; this is an approximation and should be verified if a direct HPB measurement becomes available. GI of 15 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. The dish is classified as keto-compatible given carbohydrate content of 2.6 g/100g. Topping selection affects the final nutritional profile; values represent the base dish with four standard vegetable toppings as reported by HPB. No multilingual local names are required for this Western cuisine dish; local_names fields are null by design. improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'composite' to 'adapted' to address G5 hard gate failure.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent' to conform strictly to the allowed enumeration ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as flagged by the validator. The value 'adapted' is not in the allowed set; 'closest_parent' is the correct term for a parent-level FoodEx2 code used as the nearest defensible match.
